title: Double Selected Values via Reference Array
prompt_md:
## Goal
Store references to three separate `int` variables in a `std::array` of `std::reference_wrapper<int>`, then modify the original variables *through the array*. Doubling the first `k` wrapped values must change the originals.

## Input
- Line 1: three integers `a b c`.
- Line 2: an integer `k` (1 <= k <= 3) -- how many of the three values to double, starting from the first.

## Output
One line with the final values of `a`, `b`, `c` (space-separated). The first `k` are doubled; the rest unchanged.

## Example
Input `5 10 15` then `2` produces `10 20 15`.

test_cases.label: Sample -- double first two of three

title: Sort Three Variables via Reference Wrappers
prompt_md:
## Goal
Read three integers into separate variables, place `std::reference_wrapper`s to them in a `std::array`, then sort the *underlying values* in ascending order by swapping through the wrappers. Because the wrappers refer to the original variables, sorting through them must reorder the originals in place.

## Input
One line with three integers `x y z` (each from -1000 to 1000).

## Output
Two lines:
1. `Sorted: <smallest> <middle> <largest>` -- read back through the reference array.
2. `Vars: <x> <y> <z>` -- the variables printed in declaration order, showing they were modified in place.

## Example
Input `30 20 10` produces:
```
Sorted: 10 20 30
Vars: 10 20 30
```
